item.title: Govt must revamp technical education to match needs
item.description: THE pledges that successivegovernmentshave made since independence to bolster technical and vocational education have failed to make any mark. Challenges, meanwhile, keep surfacing, harming the objective of producing skilled human resources. The situation has come to a pass where institutions for technical education are struggling with poor enrolment......

item.title: Air pollution demands urgent comprehensive action
item.description: AIR pollution has for long been a major public health threat, but the authorities have failed to take any comprehensive measure to contain what experts call the silent epidemic. A recent research on air quality analysis in six cities in 2013–2021 by public health and informatics department at Jahangirnagar University says that long-term exposure to fine particulate matter......

item.title: Ensuring safer highways
item.description: EVERY week Bangladesh loses more than a hundred lives on its roads, and every week we call these deaths accidents. Most of them are nothing of the sort. They result from decisions that were made, or never made, far from the crash site: an intersection built without thought, a no-parking rule never enforced, a highway opened without a shoulder. The crash itself takes......

item.title: Madhupur’s green growth frontier
item.description: Bangladesh’s agricultural story has long been told through rice fields, monsoon waters and the national struggle to feed a dense population from shrinking land. That story remains important, but it is no longer sufficient. The next chapter must be about nutrition, climate resilience, ecological restoration and rural value addition. In that transition, the Madhupur Tract......

item.title: Why WASH must anchor climate policy
item.description: BANGLADESH has long been recognised as one of the world’s leading voices on climate resilience. At international climate forums in Brussels, Geneva and Dubai, the country has consistently argued for climate justice, green transitions, carbon markets and stronger financing mechanisms to address loss and damage. Yet, as Bangladesh embarks on an important......

item.title: More people around world favour China over US: study
item.description: China is now viewed more positively than the US in many countries around the world, according to a new study by the Pew Research Center. It is the first time the organisation has recorded such results, BBConline reported....Politics(Right)

item.title: Repairing doctor-patient relationship
item.description: LONG before sophisticated diagnostic equipment, artificial intelligence or precision medicine entered clinical practice, the relationship between doctor and patient formed the basis of effective treatment. A patient who trusts a physician is more likely to seek timely care, disclose symptoms honestly and comply with treatment. Equally, a doctor who enjoys public......

item.title: IMF raises concerns over banking instability, revenue
item.description: The visiting fact-finding mission of the International Monetary Fund on Thursday flagged concerns over revenue collection and banking sector instability amid high inflation, warning that the situation could worsen due to the escalating war in the Middle East....

item.title: Argentina smelt ‘blood in water’: Scaloni
item.description: Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni saluted his team’s never-say-die mentality after another come-from-behind victory on Wednesday powered them past England into the World Cup final......

item.title: Bangladesh must find an answer to pace
item.description: The format changes, but Bangladesh batters’ inability to handle the pace doesn’t, as it’s been evident so far during their ongoing Zimbabwe tour......Soccer

item.title: July Martyrs’ Day observed with call for justice, reforms
item.description: July Martyrs’ Day was observed across the country on Thursday with calls for implementing the July National Charter 2025 and building a society free from discrimination....

item.title: England fans forlorn after defeat to old foe Argentina
item.description: England fans were left crestfallen after the team again failed to reach a World Cup final, with expectant hordes glued to TV screens nationwide despondent after Wednesday’s semi-final loss to bitter rivals Argentina......

item.title: Floods damage 27,000 hectares of crops
item.description: Heavy rainfall and flash floods have damaged standing crops on 27,011 hectares of farmland, affecting the livelihoods of more than 2.11 lakh farmers in seven districts of the country....

item.title: Govt postpones remaining HSC, equivalent exams in Ctg division
item.description: The ongoing Higher Secondary Certificate and equivalent examinations in all districts under the Chattogram Division will remain postponed until further notice, according to the Ministry of Education....News

item.title: IUB launches supply chain management major
item.description: Independent University, Bangladesh launched its new supply chain management major through a programme titled Bridging Academia and Industry: Meet| Connect| Lead at IUB in Dhaka on Thursday....

item.title: BUFT observes July Martyrs’ Day
item.description: BGMEA University of Fashion and Technology observed July Martyrs’ Day 2026, paying tribute to the martyrs of the July uprising and honouring the sacrifice of Shaheed Selim Talukder, at the BUFT Multipurpose Hall in Dhaka on Thursday....News

item.title: Art installation renews calls for justice for IU student murder
item.description: A fine arts department student at the Islamic University staged a symbolic art installation on the campus in Kushtia on Thursday, demanding justice for fellow student Sajid Abdullah, alleging that little progress has been made in the murder case nearly a year after his death....

item.title: BAFWWA provides treatment to flood victims in Moulvibazar
item.description: The Bangladesh Air Force Women’s Welfare Association extended its support to flood victims in Moulvibazar on Wednesday....

item.title: Govt committed to religious freedom: minister
item.description: Thegovernmentremains committed to safeguarding religious freedom and sectarian harmony for citizens of the country, said fisheries, livestock and agriculture minister Mohammed Aminur Rashid. ...

item.title: NSU holds Meet the Parents for new batch
item.description: The department of architecture at North South University has recently organised an event titled Meet the Parents for the new students of the batch 262 at NSU in Dhaka....GeographicReference

item.title: BGB DG launches tree plantation prog at Pilkhana
item.description: Director general of the Border Guard Bangladesh Major General Mohammad Ashrafuzzaman Siddiqui inaugurated the BGB’s week-long tree plantation programme 2026 at Pilkhana in Dhaka on Thursday....

item.title: Govt to eliminate discrimination in July uprising spirit: UGC member
item.description: The core spirit of the July uprising was the aspiration of the youth to build a discrimination-free Bangladesh and the government is actively implementing initiatives to realise this vision, said University Grants Commission of Bangladesh member Professor Abdullah-Al-Mamun....

item.title: Contract signed for hiring EARN project’s service providers
item.description: The Department of Youth Development, under the Ministry of Youth and Sports, signed agreements on Thursday with several non-governmental organisations to implement the World Bank-assisted Economic Acceleration and Resilience for NEET project....

item.title: Journalists need financial security for ethical practice: minister
item.description: Information and broadcasting minister Zahir Uddin Swapon said that implementing the Ninth Wage Board was required to ensure professional security, decent salaries and allowances and job stability of the journalists....Demographics

item.title: Stronger local bodies urged to curb human trafficking
item.description: Strengthening counter-trafficking committees across Bangladesh is needed to combat human trafficking and migrant smuggling, said stakeholders at a conference on Thursday....

item.title: Nat’l Youth Day now on Nov 1
item.description: The government on Thursday revised an interim regime decision to observe the National Youth Day on August 12 and reintroduced November 1 for celebration of the day....
